content.problemStatement: Given an alphanumeric string made up of digits and lower case Latin characters only, find the sum of all the digit characters in the string.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a non‑empty alphanumeric string S consisting only of lower‑case letters and digits.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the sum of all digit characters present in the corresponding string.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Digits are 1, 2, 3 and 1; their sum is 1+2+3+1 = 7.
No digit characters, so the sum is 0.

hidden.label: general case with zeros and high digits

solution.approach: For each test case iterate over the characters of S. If a character c satisfies '0' <= c <= '9', convert it to its numeric value (c - '0') and add it to a running sum. After processing the whole string output the sum. This linear scan is optimal because every character must be examined at least once.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total length of all strings) — each character is inspected once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) additional space besides the input string.
